scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Architectural Anti-Pattern in Code Review
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Cross-service direct database access clearly identified as architectural violation in critical_findings[0], (2) Escalation routed to MJ with specific description of the service boundary ownership issue, (3) Race condition in courier selection identified in critical_findings[1], (4) Kobe does not attempt to rewrite the architecture — recommends API approach but escalates the architectural decision to MJ, (5) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Excellent identification of the cross-service DB coupling with detailed impact analysis (schema coupling, connection pool exhaustion, credential leakage)
Escalation correctly routed to MJ with blocked criteria and recommendation that MJ define the inter-service communication contract before shipping
Race condition clearly explained with reproduction steps and fix options
Bonus finding: identified that courier status is never updated after assignment — a guaranteed bug beyond just the race condition
Appropriately scoped — Kobe flags the architectural fix direction but defers the actual decision to MJ
Production readiness section correctly flags credential leakage across service boundaries as an auth/security concern
